title: Make Scaled SVD Objects
text: Use data international demographic databases create objects describe regularities demographic rates. bssvd supports package bage, intended used directly data analysis.

title: Make Scaled SVD Objects
code: devtools::install_github("bayesiandemography/bssvd")

title: Make Scaled SVD Objects
code: library(bssvd) library(rsdmx) url <- paste("https://sdmx.oecd.org/public/rest/data",              "OECD.ELS.SAE,DSD_LFS@DF_LFS_INDIC,1.1",              "all?dimensionAtObservation=AllDimensions",              sep = "/") lfp_sdmx <- rsdmx::readSDMX(url) lfp_df <- as.data.frame(lfp_sdmx) lfp <- data_ssvd_lfp(lfp_df)

title: Obtain Coefficients from Scaled SVD of Census School Attendance Data — coef_csa
text: Obtain coefficients scaled SVD school attendance data. attendance data derived population censuses United Nations Statistics Division. data available "Population 5 24 years age school attendance, sex urban/rural residence" table Population Censuses' Datasets. coefficients scaled version \(U\) matrix SVD.

code: coef_csa(data, n_comp = 5, eps = 1e-05)

title: Obtain Coefficients from Scaled SVD of Census School Attendance Data — coef_csa
text: data Data frame attendance data. (Data multiple files combined, otherwise unprocessed). n_comp Number SVD components include result. Default 5. eps Parameter truncating probabililities. Default 0.00001.

text: tibble

title: Obtain Coefficients from Scaled SVD of Census School Attendance Data — coef_csa

code: coef_himd(   zipfile,   time_interval = 1,   measure_type = c("rate", "prob"),   n_comp = 5,   eps = 1e-05 )

title: Obtain Coefficients from Scaled SVD of HIMD Data — coef_himd
text: zipfile name zipped file downloaded Human Internal Migration Database. path name handled utils::unzip(). time_interval Length interval values calculated. Choices 1 5. Default 1. measure_type "prob" "rate". Whether values treated probabilities rates. See data_ssvd_himd() details. Default "rate". n_comp Number SVD components include result. Default 5. eps Parameter truncating probabililities rates. Default 0.00001.

title: Obtain Coefficients from Scaled SVD of HIMD Data — coef_himd
text: tibble

title: Obtain Coefficients from Scaled SVD of HIMD Data — coef_himd
text: measure_type "prob", reported probabilities outside (eps, 1-eps) shifted eps 1-eps, values logit-transformed, SVD applied. measure_type "rate", reported probabilities less eps shifted eps, values log-transformed, SVD applied.

title: Obtain Coefficients from Scaled SVD of HIMD Data — coef_himd
code: zipfile <- system.file("extdata", "himd_20241023_subset.zip",                        package = "bssvd") coef_himd(zipfile) #> # A tibble: 55 × 4 #>    country_orig_dest                  time component     coef #>    <chr>                             <int> <chr>        <dbl> #>  1 Trinidad and Tobago.780010.780020  1999 Component 1  0.280 #>  2 Trinidad and Tobago.780010.780080  1999 Component 1 -1.92  #>  3 Trinidad and Tobago.780010.780094  1999 Component 1  0.226 #>  4 Trinidad and Tobago.780020.780010  1999 Component 1  0.809 #>  5 Trinidad and Tobago.780020.780080  1999 Component 1 -1.79  #>  6 Trinidad and Tobago.780020.780094  1999 Component 1  0.718 #>  7 Trinidad and Tobago.780080.780010  1999 Component 1  0.281 #>  8 Trinidad and Tobago.780080.780020  1999 Component 1  0.582 #>  9 Trinidad and Tobago.780080.780094  1999 Component 1  0.718 #> 10 Trinidad and Tobago.780094.780010  1999 Component 1  0.711 #> # ℹ 45 more rows

Default 0.00001.

text: tibble

title: Prepare Census School Attendance Data — data_ssvd_csa
text: Step 1: Download data Go Population Censuses' Datasets database, table "Population 15 years age educational attainment, age sex". Go download tab, download. 100,000 rows can downloaded one time needs done multiple steps. Step 2: Combine datasets Read downloadeded files, combine single data frame. Step 3: Call function data_ssvd_csa()
code: csa_data <- data_ssvd_ssvd(data)

what: Prepare Data from the Human Fertility Database — data_ssvd_hfd
title: Prepare Data from the Human Fertility Database — data_ssvd_hfd
text: Process age-specific fertility data Human Fertility Database ready create scaled SVD.

title: Prepare Data from the Human Fertility Database — data_ssvd_hfd
code: data_ssvd_hfd(   data,   age_min_max = 15,   age_max_min = 50,   n_comp = 5,   eps = 1e-05 )

title: Prepare Data from the Human Fertility Database — data_ssvd_hfd
text: data data frame containing HFD data. age_min_max, age_max_min Every age classification must least span range [age_min_max, age_max_min). Defaults 15 50. n_comp Number SVD components include result. Default 5. eps Floor rates. Default 0.00001.

title: Prepare Data from the Human Fertility Database — data_ssvd_hfd
text: tibble

title: Prepare Data from the Human Fertility Database — data_ssvd_hfd
text: Step 1: Download data Register log Human Fertility Database, go page ZippedDataFiles. Go "statistic" table, download file "Age-specific fertility rate" row. Step 2: Extract asfrRR.txt Extract file asfrRR.txt downloaded data, read contents,   Step 3: Call function data_ssvd_hfd()
code: asfr <- readr::read_table("asfrRR.txt", skip = 2) hfd_data <- data_ssvd_hfd(asfr)

title: Prepare Data from the Human Fertility Database — data_ssvd_hfd
text: original HFD data contains age groups "12-" (ie 12 younger) "55+" (ie 55 older). treat closed intervals "12" "55". age classification return value covers narrower range data , youngest age group return value includes ASFRs younger age groups data, oldest age group return value includes ASFRs older age groups data. instance, classification return value starts age 15, data starts age 12, ASFR age 15 includes ASFRs ages 12, 13, 14. shifting ASFRs common analyses fertility.

title: Prepare Data from the Human Fertility Database — data_ssvd_hfd
text: Rates eps shifted eps, rates log-transformed, SVD applied.

title: Prepare Data from the Human Fertility Database — data_ssvd_hfd
code: data <- data_ssvd_hfd(asfr_subset) #> Tidying data... #> Carrying out SVD for 'total'... data #> # A tibble: 34 × 5 #>    type  labels_age labels_sexgender matrix         offset     #>    <chr> <list>     <list>           <list>         <list>     #>  1 total <chr [38]> <NULL>           <dgCMatrx[,5]> <dbl [38]> #>  2 total <chr [39]> <NULL>           <dgCMatrx[,5]> <dbl [39]> #>  3 total <chr [40]> <NULL>           <dgCMatrx[,5]> <dbl [40]> #>  4 total <chr [41]> <NULL>           <dgCMatrx[,5]> <dbl [41]> #>  5 total <chr [42]> <NULL>           <dgCMatrx[,5]> <dbl [42]> #>  6 total <chr [43]> <NULL>           <dgCMatrx[,5]> <dbl [43]> #>  7 total <chr [44]> <NULL>           <dgCMatrx[,5]> <dbl [44]> #>  8 total <chr [37]> <NULL>           <dgCMatrx[,5]> <dbl [37]> #>  9 total <chr [38]> <NULL>           <dgCMatrx[,5]> <dbl [38]> #> 10 total <chr [39]> <NULL>           <dgCMatrx[,5]> <dbl [39]> #> # ℹ 24 more rows

what: Prepare Data from Human Internal Migration Database — data_ssvd_himd
title: Prepare Data from Human Internal Migration Database — data_ssvd_himd
text: Process data age-specific migration probabilities Human Internal Migration Database.

title: Prepare Data from Human Internal Migration Database — data_ssvd_himd
code: data_ssvd_himd(   zipfile,   time_interval = 1,   measure_type = c("rate", "prob"),   n_comp = 5,   eps = 1e-05 )

title: Prepare Data from Human Internal Migration Database — data_ssvd_himd
text: zipfile name zipped file downloaded Human Internal Migration Database. path name handled utils::unzip(). time_interval Length interval values calculated. Choices 1 5. Default 1. measure_type "prob" "rate". Whether values treated probabilities rates. See data_ssvd_himd() details. Default "rate". n_comp Number SVD components include result. Default 5. eps Parameter truncating probabililities rates. Default 0.00001.

title: Prepare Data from Human Internal Migration Database — data_ssvd_himd
text: tibble

title: Prepare Data from Human Internal Migration Database — data_ssvd_himd
text: Step 1: Download data Download data Dyrting, S. (2024, October 23). Data : Estimating Complete Migration Probabilities Grouped Data https://osf.io/vmrfk/. data "Migration" folder "Files" tab. facility downloading files appear working, CSV files can downloaded one one. Zip folder containing CSV files. **Step 2: Call function data_ssvd_himd() Create three datasets:
code: himd_data_rate <- data_svd_himd(zipfile = "himd.zip",                                 time_interval = 1,                                 measure_type = "rate") himd_data_prob1 <- data_svd_himd(zipfile = "himd.zip",                                  time_interval = 1,                                  measure_type = "prob") himd_data_prob5 <- data_svd_himd(zipfile = "himd.zip",                                  time_interval = 5,                                  measure_type = "prob")

title: Prepare Data from Human Internal Migration Database — data_ssvd_himd
text: original data probabilities. However, data_ssvd_himd() (coef_himd()) allow values time_interval 1 treated rates. one change residence year unusual, one-year probabilities fact similar.

title: Prepare Data from Human Internal Migration Database — data_ssvd_himd
code: zipfile <- system.file("extdata", "himd_20241023_subset.zip",                        package = "bssvd") data_ssvd_himd(zipfile) #> Unzipping file... #> Creating five-year age groups... #> Assembling datasets for alternative open age groups... #> Carrying out SVD... #> # A tibble: 22 × 5 #>    type  labels_age  labels_sexgender matrix         offset      #>    <chr> <list>      <list>           <list>         <list>      #>  1 total <chr [61]>  <NULL>           <dgCMatrx[,5]> <dbl [61]>  #>  2 total <chr [66]>  <NULL>           <dgCMatrx[,5]> <dbl [66]>  #>  3 total <chr [71]>  <NULL>           <dgCMatrx[,5]> <dbl [71]>  #>  4 total <chr [76]>  <NULL>           <dgCMatrx[,5]> <dbl [76]>  #>  5 total <chr [81]>  <NULL>           <dgCMatrx[,5]> <dbl [81]>  #>  6 total <chr [86]>  <NULL>           <dgCMatrx[,5]> <dbl [86]>  #>  7 total <chr [91]>  <NULL>           <dgCMatrx[,5]> <dbl [91]>  #>  8 total <chr [96]>  <NULL>           <dgCMatrx[,5]> <dbl [96]>  #>  9 total <chr [101]> <NULL>           <dgCMatrx[,5]> <dbl [101]> #> 10 total <chr [106]> <NULL>           <dgCMatrx[,5]> <dbl [106]> #> # ℹ 12 more rows

code: data_ssvd_hmd(   zipfile,   n_comp = 5,   date = c("2025-09-25", "2024-02-26"),   year_min = NULL,   eps = 1e-05 )

title: Prepare Data from Human Mortality Database — data_ssvd_hmd
text: zipfile name zipped file downloaded Human Mortality Database. path name handled utils::unzip(). n_comp Number SVD components include result. Default 5. date Date data produced. Choices "2025-09-25", "2024-02-26". (Class character, Date.) year_min include data year_min onwards. Ignored year_min NULL (default). eps Floor rates. Default 0.00001.

title: Prepare Data from Human Mortality Database — data_ssvd_hmd
text: tibble

title: Prepare Data from Human Mortality Database — data_ssvd_hmd
text: Step 1: Download data Register log Human Mortality Database, go page Downloading HMD zipped data files. Go "Previous Versions" table bottom page, download file "Statistics" column, eg file https://www.mortality.org/File/Download/hmd.v6/zip/all_hmd/hmd_statistics_20240226.zip Step 2: Call function data_ssvd_hmd() Put file working directory, supply name file function data_ssvd_hmd(), eg
code: hmd_data <- data_ssvd_hmd("hmd_statistcs_20240226")

title: Prepare Data from Human Mortality Database — data_ssvd_hmd
text: Rates eps shifted eps, rates log-transformed, SVD applied.

title: Prepare Data from Human Mortality Database — data_ssvd_hmd
code: zipfile <- system.file("extdata", "hmd_statistics_20250925_subset.zip",                        package = "bssvd") data <- data_ssvd_hmd(zipfile) #> Unzipping file... #> Tidying data... #> Creating five-year age groups... #> Assembling datasets for alternative open age groups... #> Carrying out SVD for 'total'... #> Carrying out SVD for 'indep'... #> Carrying out SVD for 'joint'... #> Combining results... data #> # A tibble: 99 × 5 #>    type  labels_age  labels_sexgender matrix         offset      #>    <chr> <list>      <list>           <list>         <list>      #>  1 total <chr [61]>  <NULL>           <dgCMatrx[,5]> <dbl [61]>  #>  2 total <chr [66]>  <NULL>           <dgCMatrx[,5]> <dbl [66]>  #>  3 total <chr [71]>  <NULL>           <dgCMatrx[,5]> <dbl [71]>  #>  4 total <chr [76]>  <NULL>           <dgCMatrx[,5]> <dbl [76]>  #>  5 total <chr [81]>  <NULL>           <dgCMatrx[,5]> <dbl [81]>  #>  6 total <chr [86]>  <NULL>           <dgCMatrx[,5]> <dbl [86]>  #>  7 total <chr [91]>  <NULL>           <dgCMatrx[,5]> <dbl [91]>  #>  8 total <chr [96]>  <NULL>           <dgCMatrx[,5]> <dbl [96]>  #>  9 total <chr [101]> <NULL>           <dgCMatrx[,5]> <dbl [101]> #> 10 total <chr [106]> <NULL>           <dgCMatrx[,5]> <dbl [106]> #> # ℹ 89 more rows

what: Prepare OECD Data on Labour Force Participation Rates — data_ssvd_lfp
title: Prepare OECD Data on Labour Force Participation Rates — data_ssvd_lfp
text: Process labour force participation data downloaded OECD Data Explorer ready create scaled SVD.

title: Prepare OECD Data on Labour Force Participation Rates — data_ssvd_lfp
code: data_ssvd_lfp(data, age_max = 75, n_comp = 5, year_min = NULL, eps = 1e-05)

title: Prepare OECD Data on Labour Force Participation Rates — data_ssvd_lfp
text: data data frame containing OECD data. age_max upper limit oldest closed age group. Default 75. n_comp Number SVD components include result. Default 5. year_min include data year_min onwards. Ignored year_min NULL (default). eps Parameter truncating probabililities rates. Default 0.00001.

title: Prepare OECD Data on Labour Force Participation Rates — data_ssvd_lfp
text: tibble

title: Prepare OECD Data on Labour Force Participation Rates — data_ssvd_lfp
text: Step 1: Download data   Step 2: Call function data_ssvd_lfp()
code: library(rsdmx) url <- paste("https://sdmx.oecd.org/public/rest/data",              "OECD.ELS.SAE,DSD_LFS@DF_LFS_INDIC,1.1",              "all?dimensionAtObservation=AllDimensions",              sep = "/") lfp_sdmx <- rsdmx::readSDMX(url)    ## can be slow lfp_df <- as.data.frame(lfp_sdmx)   ## can also be slow lfp_data <- data_ssvd_lfp(data)

title: Prepare OECD Data on Labour Force Participation Rates — data_ssvd_lfp
code: data <- data_ssvd_lfp(oecd_lfp_subset) #> Tidying data... #> Carrying out SVD for 'total'... #> Carrying out SVD for 'indep'... #> Carrying out SVD for 'joint'... #> Combining results... data #> # A tibble: 18 × 5 #>    type  labels_age labels_sexgender matrix          offset     #>    <chr> <list>     <list>           <list>          <list>     #>  1 total <chr [10]> <NULL>           <dgCMatrx[,5]>  <dbl [10]> #>  2 total <chr [11]> <NULL>           <dgCMatrx[,5]>  <dbl [11]> #>  3 total <chr [12]> <NULL>           <dgCMatrx[,5]>  <dbl [12]> #>  4 total <chr [11]> <NULL>           <dgCMatrx[,5]>  <dbl [11]> #>  5 total <chr [12]> <NULL>           <dgCMatrx[,5]>  <dbl [12]> #>  6 total <chr [13]> <NULL>           <dgCMatrx[,5]>  <dbl [13]> #>  7 indep <chr [20]> <chr [20]>       <dgTMatrx[,10]> <dbl [20]> #>  8 indep <chr [22]> <chr [22]>       <dgTMatrx[,10]> <dbl [22]> #>  9 indep <chr [24]> <chr [24]>       <dgTMatrx[,10]> <dbl [24]> #> 10 indep <chr [22]> <chr [22]>       <dgTMatrx[,10]> <dbl [22]> #> 11 indep <chr [24]> <chr [24]>       <dgTMatrx[,10]> <dbl [24]> #> 12 indep <chr [26]> <chr [26]>       <dgTMatrx[,10]> <dbl [26]> #> 13 joint <chr [20]> <chr [20]>       <dgCMatrx[,5]>  <dbl [20]> #> 14 joint <chr [22]> <chr [22]>       <dgCMatrx[,5]>  <dbl [22]> #> 15 joint <chr [24]> <chr [24]>       <dgCMatrx[,5]>  <dbl [24]> #> 16 joint <chr [22]> <chr [22]>       <dgCMatrx[,5]>  <dbl [22]> #> 17 joint <chr [24]> <chr [24]>       <dgCMatrx[,5]>  <dbl [24]> #> 18 joint <chr [26]> <chr [26]>       <dgCMatrx[,5]>  <dbl [26]>

title: Process World Marriage Data — data_ssvd_wmd
text: file Path excel spreadsheet holding World Marriage Data n_comp Number SVD components include result. Default 5. status "current" (default) "ever". Whether report results marriage staus "currently married" "ever-married". Note statuses include consensual de facto unions, addition legal marriages. eps Parameter truncating probabililities rates. Default 0.00001.

code: wmd_data <- data_ssvd_wmd("undesa_pd_2019_wmd_marital_status.xlsx")

what: Create Components Needed by a Scaled SVD Object — make_matrix_and_offset
title: Create Components Needed by a Scaled SVD Object — make_matrix_and_offset
text: Use Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) construct parsimonious representation set rates, probabilities, means, values. construction proceeds follows: shift values outside permitted range; transform values matrix x (eg take logs); carry SVD transformed version x; centre scale results SVD produce matrix matrix vector offset.

title: Create Components Needed by a Scaled SVD Object — make_matrix_and_offset
code: make_matrix_and_offset(x, transform, n_comp = 10, eps = 1e-05)

title: Create Components Needed by a Scaled SVD Object — make_matrix_and_offset
text: x matrix value rates, probabilities, means. transform "log", "logit", "none". n_comp Number components. eps Quantity used setting floor ceiling value. See details. Default 0.00001.

title: Create Components Needed by a Scaled SVD Object — make_matrix_and_offset
text: named list two elements: matrix, numeric matrix offset, numeric vector

title: Create Components Needed by a Scaled SVD Object — make_matrix_and_offset
text: \(X\) matrix transformed values x \(F\) matrix called matrix, \(g\) vector called offset, \(\alpha\) vector standard normal variates, $$\beta = F \alpha + g$$ \(\beta\) look like randomly-selected column \(X\). Matrix x typically age along rows, combination classification variables, country time, along columns. One exception SVD used capture relationship female male rates, case rows formed interacting sex age. See example. transform "log" "logit", make_matrix_and_offset() converts values x less eps values greater equal eps transforming. converted value based main effects model. transform "logit", make_matrix_and_offset() converts values greater 1-epsto1-eps` transforming. converted value based main effects model.

title: Create Components Needed by a Scaled SVD Object — make_matrix_and_offset

text: tibble 12,069 rows columns TIME_PERIOD, REF_AREA, MEASURE,UNIT_MEASURE, SEX, AGE, LABOUR FORCE STATUS, obsValue, DECIMALS, OBS_STATUS.

title: OECD Data on Labour Force Participation — oecd_lfp_subset
text: Downloaded table "Employment unemployment five-year age group sex - indicators" OECD Data Explorer. Accessed 21 April 2024. subset data included oecd_lfp_subset.

text: labour force participation rate number people labour force (ie number people currently employed unemployed) proportion population.

what: Calculate the Percent of Variance Explained by Components in SVD — percent_variance
title: Calculate the Percent of Variance Explained by Components in SVD — percent_variance
text: Calculate percent variance dataset can explained component singular value decomposition. dataset include age, single sex gender category, one classifying variables.

title: Calculate the Percent of Variance Explained by Components in SVD — percent_variance
code: percent_variance(   data,   transform,   measure = "value",   cols = c("country", "time"),   n_comp = 5,   eps = 1e-05 )

title: Calculate the Percent of Variance Explained by Components in SVD — percent_variance
text: data data frame transform Transformation apply data: "log", "logit", "none". measure Name rate proportion modelled. Default "value". cols Names categorical variables used distinguish different age profiles. n_comp Number components give percentages . Default 5. eps Parameter controlling truncation. See details. Default 0.00001.

title: Calculate the Percent of Variance Explained by Components in SVD — percent_variance
text: numeric vector length n_comp.

title: Calculate the Percent of Variance Explained by Components in SVD — percent_variance
text: data must contain variable called "age". must also contain variables named measure cols arguments. age cols arguments must uniquely identify cells dataset. transform "log" "logit", percent_variance() converts values measure variable less eps values greater equal eps transforming. converted value based main effects model. transform "logit", precent_variange() converts values greater 1-epsto values less equal to1-eps` transforming. converted value based main effects model.

title: Calculate the Percent of Variance Explained by Components in SVD — percent_variance
code: asfr_subset |>   tidy_hfd() |>   percent_variance(transform = "log") #> Component 1 Component 2 Component 3 Component 4 Component 5  #>    99.29102    99.60120    99.71626    99.80913    99.86552
